era: Early 1990s-present. Hip-Hop DJ and producer. E=5 -- dual-threat producer/DJ energy. Conception Records founder. Central District roots. Three decades in Seattle hip-hop underground.

location.formative: Central District -- the geographic center of Seattle's hip-hop scene. Community network, local venues, CD culture. Vitamin D has witnessed the entire arc of gentrification from inside the CD.
location.career: Vitamin D. Producer. DJ. Conception Records founder. Central District-based. Three decades of continuous practice. Local MC collaborator. Scene architect.

members.name: Vitamin D
members.note: Central District-based producer and DJ. Conception Records. Three decades in Seattle hip-hop. Production credits across the local scene. Sample-based, crate-digging tradition. Both creates and performs. Scene architect.

associated_musicians.name: DJ Riz
associated_musicians.note: 206 Zulu. E=5. Central District turntablist. Vitamin D (138) continues the CD geographic thread.

associated_musicians.name: DJ Revolution
associated_musicians.note: Stones Throw. E=6. National reach. Vitamin D (138, E=5) returns to the CD underground after the national excursion.

associated_musicians.name: Sabzi
associated_musicians.note: Blue Scholars/Common Market. E=4. Both are Seattle hip-hop producers. Sabzi produced for specific groups; Vitamin D produces across the scene.

associated_musicians.name: Nasty Nes Rodriguez
associated_musicians.note: KCMU pioneer. E=4. The first hip-hop figure in S36. Vitamin D continues the lineage.

associated_musicians.name: DJ B-Mello
associated_musicians.note: KEXP Street Sounds. E=5. Same energy level as Vitamin D. Radio DJ vs. scene producer.

theory_nodes.concept: Producer-DJ duality -- Vitamin D as both creator (studio production) and performer (live DJ); the dual-threat model in hip-hop; production as sonic documentation of the Central District
examples: Production: sample-based beats, crate-digging, studio craft. Creating the sonic landscape.
DJ: live sets, crowd reading, set building. Performing and presenting.
The duality is synergistic: DJ ear informs production; production feeds DJ sets.
Instrumental albums: the beat as complete musical statement.
theory_nodes.description: Producer-DJ duality. Creation and performance. Vitamin D inhabits both.

ancestors: DJ Riz (136) as turntablist. DJ Revolution (137) as battle DJ.
genealogy.relationship: Vitamin D at 138 bridges the DJ role and the producer role.

theory_nodes.concept: Conception Records -- Central District hip-hop label; DIY independent; documentation and distribution platform for the Seattle underground; physical media commitment
examples: Physical media: vinyl, CD, cassette releases as artifacts.
Catalog documents the Seattle hip-hop underground across decades.
Community function: release platform, event organizer, cultural infrastructure.
theory_nodes.description: Conception Records. Central District hip-hop label. DIY independent.

ancestors: Stones Throw (137) as national independent label.
genealogy.relationship: Conception Records at 138. Local independent label. CD community anchor.

theory_nodes.concept: Central District streak continues -- DJ Riz (136), Vitamin D (138); the CD as geographic anchor of the hip-hop era; cultural persistence through gentrification
examples: DJ Riz (136): introduced the CD. 206 Zulu. Community turntablist.
DJ Revolution (137): Seattle-born, national reach. Brief CD departure.
Vitamin D (138): returns to CD. Producer/DJ. Conception Records.
Cultural persistence: Vitamin D has witnessed entire gentrification arc.
theory_nodes.description: Central District streak continues. CD as hip-hop era anchor.

ancestors: DJ Riz documented the CD introduction.
genealogy.relationship: Vitamin D at 138 deepens the CD documentation.

theory_nodes.concept: E=5 producer-performer tier -- dual-threat energy; higher than host/curator (E=3/4) but below battle competition (E=6); the sustained creative energy of production plus performance
examples: Production energy: studio craft, sustained creative work.
Performance energy: live DJ sets, crowd connection.
Combined: two skill sets, one figure. E=5 sustained intensity.
Matches DJ Riz (136, E=5) and B-Mello (134, E=5). Below Revolution (137, E=6).
theory_nodes.description: E=5 producer-DJ tier. Dual-threat sustained energy.

genealogy.relationship: E=5 producer-DJ at 138. Dual-threat returns to E=5 tier.

cross_references.relationship: CENTRAL DISTRICT! DJ Riz (136, E=5, 206 Zulu) and Vitamin D (138, E=5, Conception Records). Both CD-rooted. Both E=5. Turntablist vs. producer-DJ. Two pillars of the Central District hip-hop community.
DJ SEQUENCE! Revolution (137, E=6, Stones Throw, national) to Vitamin D (138, E=5, Conception, local). National back to local. E=6 back to E=5. The sequence oscillates between reach and roots.
SEATTLE PRODUCERS! Sabzi (122, E=4, Blue Scholars/Common Market) and Vitamin D (138, E=5, Conception Records). Both producers. Sabzi produces for specific groups; Vitamin D produces across the scene.
E=5 DJ TIER! B-Mello (134, E=5, KEXP), DJ Riz (136, E=5, 206 Zulu), Vitamin D (138, E=5, Conception). Three E=5 DJs/producers anchoring the tier.
HIP-HOP LINEAGE! Nasty Nes (126, E=4) through Vitamin D (138, E=5). The Seattle hip-hop thread through S36 continues.
